summary: The stop-hook completion path now distinguishes password-only recovery from missing-review recovery, so a valid linked receipt no longer triggers an unnecessary reviewer rerun just to echo `COMPLETION_PASSWORD`.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: The new retry helper keeps the recovery contract narrow without changing the receipt gate.
Tests now cover both valid-receipt password retries and true rerun cases.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: Password-only recovery no longer over-instructs the live lane when the linked receipt already passes.
The nonce remains scoped to active ticket-backed `impl` loops, and receipt validation still decides whether another reviewer pass is actually needed.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Verification covers 51 tests across the stop-hook and tmux-helper suites, ticket metadata validation, and diff hygiene.
The docs and policy surfaces still match the implemented password-plus-receipt contract.

next_action: Close out the ticket or archive it on the next close-ticket pass.
